I could not believe that they got it done all in ONE DAY.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marge Sicurella","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIDzQKSJd4WU3pt1khBrpDgIXlWhdWDIo7q3npqqByxBcz3yA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"After calling a couple of fencing companies and being very unimpressed with their limited suggestions or unreturned calls my husband suggested National Fence Systems.  My husband works in Linden and passes their showroom frequently.  Unsure if they would come to Monmouth County I called and was given an appointment with Michael Granat.
Michael Granat is the best sales rep I have encountered in my 30 years of being a homeowner.  He came with brochures of the fence styles they offer and welcomed me to visit their showroom.  Michael was unbelievably patient with any questions I had in deciding what fence to choose.  Our property has a steep slope on one side and backs up to protected wooded land.  My husband and I went to their showroom and were very impressed to see the quality of the fences they offer. The cost was very close to the fence estimates we were given with no options or a showroom to visit.  Michael did not hesitate to design 2 styles of privacy fencing in the front and rail fencing with wire on the back for the rear of our property.  The black wire cannot be seen from a distance which makes the fence so attractive and keeps our dog in the yard.
After the fence was installed, my husband came home and was not happy with the placement of the gate in the rear of our property.  When I called Michael, I expected to get a voicemail instead he picked up the phone immediately.  He asked me to send a photo of the fence.  He assured me that he and National Fence Systems’ goal is that we are happy with our fence.  That afternoon I got a call asking if a crew could come the next day to address our concerns.  The gate was moved to the location my husband wanted.
My husband came home and cannot stop saying how happy he is with the fence.  He insisted I write this review because he is very particular and wants other people to know how strongly we recommend Michael Granat and National Fence Systems.","Images":null,"When":""}]

I was happy that they were able to do it quickly, especially during the pandemic. I did not care for the sales person I worked with but the fence installers did a nice job. When my wooden fence needed replacement in March 2024, their price was better than another fence installer, so we decided to use them again. The wooden fence was installed in April 2024. As the year went by, there were two fence panels whose color did not match the rest of the fence. Finally in June 2025, when it became obvious that they were the only two fence panels that were changing color so drastically, I reached out to Wayne Fence through an email and included photos of the mismatched fence panels.  I did not hear back from them. I found out in August 2025 that part of my wood fence was leaning onto my neighbor’s property, and we needed to have it fixed. Starting in September, I called Wayne Fence a couple of times and was told someone would return my call and no one did. In October, I decided to email them and included the property line issue and the mismatched fence panel issue and finally heard back from someone. Someone came to my house on October 29 to look at both issues. The mismatched fence panels look so different that the man questioned whether Wayne Fence had been the ones to install those two panels. He took photos of the whole fence. The properly line issue was finally fixed on December 12. Only after asking again about the mismatched fence panels did someone tell me that the panels are different due to weathering so they won’t replace them under the warranty. I asked about these fence panels several times over our emails and nothing like that was ever stated before. Also, it seems odd that weather would cause only two panels, the last two to be installed that day so perhaps not from the same batch of material, to look so different.\n\nWhile the initial installation went up in a timely manner and was a decent price, having to get in touch with them regarding things that needed fixing has been quite frustrating.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCWsG62r9zAqzbfbqVNmmic\u0026fid=0x0:0x8df82e20f84303b"],"When":"2025-12-12"},{"Name":"Andrew Matulewicz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK1Su20LQvRb5cuyojnFThfCk5734m0hTg3zZrl03ugt9fF-g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Upgraded from chain link to Vinyl PVC. He kept me in the loop exactly when the crew/materials/etc. were arriving, so I knew who was coming/going at all times. As all job sites have surprises/issues that arise, all here were addressed with no hassles. The removal/materials crew were great. The installers, Rodolpho and Julio, were ready to go before 9am and did such a great job that I was already loving that fence while it was being installed! JOB WELL DONE! and THANK YOU.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Viviana Nunez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWd0BXckvDuGJsdxncu0zYLahxJNkg41ezZ6FVPdWtr3p5bO0E=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a great idea we had to signed up a contract and have Jeff and his team members from Wayne Fence to do our backyard. I made an appointment with Joe he showed up on time, measured, and gave his input on what styles of fence would complement my house and back yard. Joe was very detail oriented, he even took into account things such as having a double gate for landscape equipment to fit through, an access gate to service my generator and even a rear yard access gate in case the kids threw a ball over the fence!! The installation crew showed up and got my fence installed in 1 day and the job was perfect. Joe then came back to inspect the work of the installers and made sure I was 100% happy with everything before he left. The next day they came to my house to clean up where the materials were dropped in my driveway , this was unexpected and really shows how much they care about making a customer happy. Now my kids have a safe and secure backyard to run around in . Thank you again to Wayne Fence the courtesy and professionalism is unmatched! I would recommend Wayne Fence to all my family and friends they are a 5 star company. I am very big on customer service and Wayne Fence knocked it out of the park!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID2tL7zUQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x8df82e20f84303b"],"When":"2022-5-7"},{"Name":"Nichole Montenegro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKFT1hufOFjgHB77lg7AtV8-IqcHCXay8HoVH7tJjf2UtIJQA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"As the program supervisor for a Daycare Center I contacted Wayne Fence based off of the reviews read.  All initial communication was fantastic.  They followed up and followed through, sent their best people for quoting and promised exceptional work.  Once the full amount was paid for, $10,200 prior which was prior to work being started is where the issues began.  We from this point had little contact with them.  We were notified that our start date was being pushed back due to manufactures delays.  When the work did begin it was noticed that the job was extremely rushed.  After the fencing was placed we noticed that the fencing itself was extremely uneven.  The caps of the fencing posts were not properly placed and were crooked and students were able to remove them.  There were so many scratches in the a lot of fencing and posts which will cause the fence to rust due to exposure.  They did attempt to spray paint many of those imperfections which is extremely frustrating being that we paid for brand new fencing.  We expressed our concerns to reception and asked  to speak with management on more than several occasions in an attempt to give them an opportunity to fix the issues, management did not get back to us and kept pushing reception to handle conversations.  We were told someone would either call or come in person.  Every attempt they made was either rescheduled, or they did not show up when they said they would.  When they did come to \"fix\" the issues, they caused damage to the artificial turf in the playground by cutting, removing and patching up areas on their own accord.  We thought we were getting quality work and received sub par work.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICjkpDlOA\u0026fid=0x0:0x8df82e20f84303b"],"When":"2024-4-19"}]

•	There were some installation issues, including a large gap under one of the gates that my dog could easily fit through. It took some back and forth to get this resolved.
•	During the initial install, the team didn’t have the final gate with them. They were supposed to return the next day (Tuesday), but the visit was delayed twice. It didn’t seem like finishing the job was their priority after the initial install

Although they are a smaller company than some others they worked me on pricing and performed a vinyl fence install in my yard. Everything was explained well, they came when they said they would. The estimate said the job would take one day but it ended up taking 1.5 days and that was fine with me because the quality of the work was excellent. Their crew was careful and maintained a high level of quality for all aspects of the job. Highly recommended and would definitely have them do more work if I need it in the future. Everyone I spoke with in person and on the phone were very helpful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICOsNfZjwE\u0026fid=0x0:0xe8c65fdc6ea4f59d"],"When":"2022-5-30"},{"Name":"Nicole Appello","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWO9UILNiMbdZEd8OfxVHotqx532h6vzlGVU9ytrnHZ7iflvbHxdQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Cannot say enough about this company, impressive from start to finish. Kim was unbelievably helpful in making sure the permit was approved by our town. She handled a LOT of back and forth and was always pleasant, responsive, and accommodating. When the town finally approved the permit the installation was scheduled within a week. First of all, if you deal with Rob, you need to be on top of him about making sure he puts the orders in on time and that he writes the proper instructions on the order because he will sneakily write things a certain way and then say that was the only option the computer let him write it as, but don’t worry it will be done how we said. I’ll be there on the first day to explain it to the guys.  He knew I wanted an extra gate more than three weeks before project started and he forgot to add it which delayed the project an extra week, pushing back and cutting short my family vacation which time was requested and taken off from work (which can’t be changed plus lost moneys). Interestingly, I had to be the one to remind/ verify with Rob to do his job of adding the gate or else the materials would not have been ordered. Then, he promised one price and the gave another. And not even the least bit of professionalism from him to admit the mistake and try to fix. He did show up the second day (weeks since his screw up) to talk to the workers (which they told him I wanted to talk if he ever showed up) but he never approached me or rang the bell, just walked around and left. Also, the gates when originally discussed and sold to me were supposed to open out, then I was told both directions, and finally at the end of the install, on the last day, I was told they only open inwards. Also, the latch was originally sold/ told to me that it would be a nice one with handles on all gates but then on last day of install, the gate had a basic flip up latch system because that’s the only thing that works with metal post. Again not what was discussed when selling original contract.  Lastly, I was told to cut down a beautiful tall tree because  it was in the way and to have it done before the crew arrived, which turns out that it didn’t have to be cut  down at all, it wasn’t even in the way one bit. Going back to sneakily writing instructions, he originally told me that his company would clear / remove the trees behind the shed but on the day off it was just trimming because of the way Rob wrote it so I ended up cutting it myself because I wasn’t going to pay a lot of money for what was not agreed upon. And to get that money returned was a hassle. So far the company promised a lot but did not provided and live up to there end of the agreement.  When talking to Mark, the manager, he was there the first day instead of Rob, he knew more about property lines and where to place fence and contract language. But when asked to fix/ resolve his employees mistake, he used the excuse that it’s above my pay grade and we are sticking to what was written. Only saying sorry there was a miscommunication and that he will talk to Rob so this doesn’t happen again as if that fixes things like cutting down a beautiful tree, selling products that will work one way but then it’s the opposite, delaying vacations and problems that go with it, giving cheap latch systems when promised quality system with handles, etc. But I have to tell you they were quick, speedy, on time, and remembered  to make sure to send emails to collect the the balance of the money.  Now that they have good service on.  The one good thing about the project was the subcontractors. They were very hard workers, knowledgeable, experienced, and reliable. I had an issue with one panel x2 after high winds. They came out to repair this time gratis and quickly. Appreciate their service and follow up!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bill","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXN-QiWIUMiQSl8gMlRrMW2iruJE8MO7PNaeKRsYue9A4FI8akA4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If you are looking for a fence company that not only does a great job, but stands by their work, definitely look elsewhere. We had 300’ of solid wood fence installed in 2014, by 2018 a couple of the sections were starting to lean. This past year, a couple of the fence panels had fallen over and that’s where I discovered they were improperly installed. For a 6’ high solid wood fence, you are supposed to have 9’ pressure treated 4x4s supporting them; and that’s what we paid for. What I found out after the panels fell was that there are multiple posts that are much less than the required 9’. On 1 panel, the post was only 11\" into the ground (see pic below) meaning they cut off over 2 feet of what they said was absolutely necessary to support these panels. At this point there are multiple sections that are leaning over and need the posts replaced correctly. I called Taylor fence this past summer and they sent someone out to inspect the fence. The person they sent out agreed that that the fence posts were not properly installed and was the cause of the fence failure. It was noted that the posts were likely cut due to roots but that the install was not properly done. He also noted that a cedar fence should not be directly in contact with the ground; well at least half of my fence is on the ground and there is noticeable rot. After a few weeks, the same person came back to my house to tell me that he was told by the company (Taylors)  that the panels/posts were properly installed. A short time later I get an estimate of $540 for replacing 1 fence panel and 3 posts. At the time of this review I have discovered 2 more fence posts that are only standing because they are being supported inline with the rest of the fence. For a fence that is supposed to last at least 20 years (according to them), I'll be lucky to get 10.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICK-u7KOg\u0026fid=0x0:0xaf1f976600143c33"],"When":"2021-4-2"}]
